name: ISO/IEC 27001 Information Security Management
description: International standard for establishing, implementing, maintaining and continually improving an information security management system (ISMS). Encoded at clause and Annex A theme level.

principles.title: Context of the organization
principles.summary: The organisation must determine internal and external issues, interested parties, and the scope of the ISMS.
queries: scope of the information security management system
stakeholders and context for information security
principles.expects: The document defines the scope and context of the security programme.

principles.title: Leadership and policy
principles.summary: Top management must demonstrate leadership, establish an information security policy, and assign roles and responsibilities.
queries: information security policy approved by management
principles.expects: The document shows management commitment, a security policy, and assigned responsibilities.

principles.title: Planning and risk management
principles.summary: Risk assessment and risk treatment processes must be defined, with security objectives and a Statement of Applicability.
queries: risk treatment plan and security objectives
principles.expects: The document describes how security risks are assessed and treated.

principles.title: Support, competence and awareness
principles.summary: Resources, competence, awareness, communication, and documented information must support the ISMS.
queries: resources and documentation for security management
principles.expects: The document covers training, awareness, and documentation supporting security.

principles.title: Operation
principles.summary: Operational planning and control, including performing risk assessments and implementing treatment plans.
principles.expects: The document describes operational security procedures in practice.

principles.title: Performance evaluation
principles.summary: Monitoring, measurement, internal audit, and management review of the ISMS.
principles.expects: The document provides for monitoring, audits, and management review of security.

principles.title: Improvement
principles.summary: Nonconformities must be corrected and the ISMS continually improved.
principles.expects: The document commits to corrective actions and continual improvement.

principles.title: Organizational controls
principles.summary: Policies, asset management, access control governance, supplier relationships, incident management, and compliance.
queries: asset inventory and acceptable use policies
supplier security and incident management process
principles.expects: The document addresses organisational controls such as asset management, supplier security, and incident handling.

principles.title: People controls
principles.summary: Screening, terms of employment, awareness, disciplinary process, and responsibilities after termination.
principles.expects: The document covers personnel security measures across the employment lifecycle.

principles.title: Physical controls
principles.summary: Physical security perimeters, entry controls, equipment protection, and secure disposal.
queries: physical security of offices and equipment
secure disposal of equipment and media
principles.expects: The document addresses physical protection of premises and equipment.

principles.title: Technological controls
principles.summary: Endpoint security, access rights, malware protection, backups, logging, network security, cryptography, and secure development.
principles.expects: The document describes technical controls such as backups, logging, encryption, and network security.
